description: A postsecondary psychology professor is validating a new 30-item Matrix Reasoning test. Perform a Classical Test Theory (CTT) item analysis using Python on the trial-by-trial response data at ~/pebl/data/matrix_reasoning_data.csv (columns: participant_id, item_id, response, correct_answer, correct, rt_ms). Exclude participants with a mean reaction time across all 30 items of less than 1,500 ms. For the valid participants, calculate Item Difficulty, Corrected Item-Total Correlation (Pearson correlation between an item and the sum of the other 29 items), and Cronbach's Alpha using sample variance. Identify the single miskeyed item with a negative corrected item-total correlation. Export the results for all 30 items to ~/pebl/analysis/item_analysis_report.json using the structure: {"excluded_participants": [], "cronbach_alpha": 0.0, "bad_item": {"item_id": 0, "difficulty": 0.0, "corrected_item_total_correlation": 0.0}, "items": [{"item_id": 0, "difficulty": 0.0, "corrected_item_total_correlation": 0.0}]}.

metadata.data_source: IRT-simulated (Rasch model) open-source psychometrics project data
